description: Page 13, Presbytery appointed a moderator of Session without consent of Session BCO 12-3
Page 5, 91-5 No mention of candidate for licensure actually preaching before presbytery or committee.
p. 5 Not clear if examinations are for ordination, licensure, or transfer at time of examination.

p. 7 (#1) No record BCO 19-8, 9, 10 were fulfilled in approving man for internship; #2 Not enough information on candidate to know if all of BCO 18-7 has been fulfilled. Not enough information given for men taken under care to know if BCO 18-2 has been fulfilled.
p. 8 For ordination: No record of exegetical and theological papers having been done or that sermon was preached. BCO 21-4 b.c.d. 19-2d.
p. 11 Licentiates not addressed by moderator, nor is authorized form used to record action in minutes. BCO 19-4

p. 12 1,2,3,4 Appendix D1 gives no details - when the Committee met as a commission. No record when they met, where they met, who attended, whether there is a quorum. No standing rules to determine if committee can act as a commission.
p. 1 no record of the purpose of a called meeting. RAO 14-10 d 2.
p. 4 Not enough information to know if BCO 18-2 was fulfilled.
p. 10 #8 Presbytery ordained a man, but not all elements of BCO 21-4 were done. Also do not know to what ministry he is called.
p. 11 No indication that candidates were addressed according to BCO 19-4.
p. 122 Not clear if examinations are for ordination, licensure or transfer at time of examination.
p. 3 Commission report not adopted by presbytery, 15-1, not dismissed.
p. 5 Proper form for licensure not given 19:4
p. 6 A candidate for ordination not examined here or earlier on church history. BCO 21-4a7
p. 6 Commission's report not adopted, Appendix lacks proper information.
p. 7 No quorum listed for commission BCO 15-1
p. 8 Presbytery violated BCO 34-10 in not following up on absent TE for 7 years. Used wrong provision of BCO to remove him from office. BCO 34-10 applies not 38-2
p. 13 No listing of exceptions taken to sessional records.
RAO 14-10 h No directory, roll, or standing rules.
Page 6 48-8.F 7) 9) No record on candidates completing exegesis and theology papers or having completed internship for ordination. BCO 21-4
49-9.2 No record of candidate for ordination completing exegesis and theology papers or having completed internship BCO 21-4
50-10 B.3;4.a No mention of theology or exegetical papers for those being ordained and no mention of written sermon for licentiates BCO 21-4; 19-2d
51-10.c 3) Commission consisting of 3 TE and 1 RE contrary to BCO 15-2
Page 2 53-12.A.4 BCO 45-5 Protest was not recorded, no explanation given.
RAO 14-10 h. No directory, roll, list of candidates or licentiates, no standing rules.
No report on interns BCO 19-12 in any minutes

BCO 43-1-p. 92 Presbytery improperly ruled the complaint of Grace Presbyterian Church out of order. Rationale: The complaint was against the action of Presbytery in receiving a protest rather than the protest itself. It is proper to register a complaint against an action of presbytery. The phrase 'Here the matter shall end...' (BCO 45-4) cited by presbytery in support of its action refers to the subject matter of a protest and not the act of receiving the protest.
Page 7, 70-76 & 79. Actions of Presbytery contradictory and unclear.

90-11 Unclear if Presbytery licensed stated supply as required in BCO 19-1. There is no record of the examination parts being done. BCO 19-2. There is no record of a minister from another denomination answering the questions of BCO 19-3.
90-40J The Presbytery cannot delegate the task of examining a person for licensure to a commission. There is no record of such commission having voted as a commission. BCO 19-2
GENERAL: No attachments to minutes included. BCO 13-10.

Page 12, 10.2 A candidate for licensure's exception to the standards (it is not necessary to observe the whole day as holy unto the Lord) compromises a fundamental of our system of doctrine. WCF 21-8, LC 115-120, SC 57-62, BCO 48-1-7.
Page 18, 9.2 Exception taken by a teaching elder from another denomination to standards compromises a fundamental of our system of doctrine. WCF 21-8, LC 115-120, SH 57-62, BCO 48-1-7.
Page 18, 9.3 No mention of academic qualifications or examination in original languages. BCO 21-4.
p. 264, P 1 Presbytery divided itself into 3 parts to examine two ordinands and one licentiate. This is contrary to BCO 15-2 and BCO 21-4.
No report of interns at each stated meeting.
No yearly report from interns.
No yearly report on candidates under care. BCO 21-6.

Page 5, Report of the Commission to Ordain and Install. BCO-15-1 requires approval of commission's report to become action of the court and be entered into minutes. No such approval is mentioned. Also no motion to dissolve commission.

Commission dismissed RE from service as an elder. The commission has authority to act as the session, only if this has been agreed to by the congregation. These minutes do not show whether or not this is the case. BCO 15-2, 13-9.
First reading of Standing Rules change with no vote recorded. SR p.2
p. 1 Minutes stated they are not official until approved by next stated meeting. No record of approval at next stated meeting found.
p. 30, 33-D Failure to cite Session for admitting children to communion without profession of faith. (BCO 6-2, -4 & 58-4)
p. 25 (20.1) Ordination exam should not be in views, but knowledge. Other than theology, no other areas examined (BCO 21-4 a-d)
No Directory, Roll, or Standing Rules included during year.
No minutes submitted
There is no record of those present at the meeting, nor is there a record that a quorum was present. RAO 14-10.C.6
Directory was incomplete - lacked listing of committees. Roll was incomplete - lacked addresses of clerks of Session. No up-to-date copy of Standing rules was included.
p. 19 #2 Commission erected to examine for licensure. BCO 19-2
BCO 19-12 No record of progress of interns included in the minutes.
BCO 15-1 No reports of commissions or actions approving those reports appear.

par. 8 Commission did not declare a quorum present, did not elect chair and clerk and did not record who closed in prayer. No record of service of installation or who fulfilled various responsibilities.
par. 15 No call approved or declaration that a TE will be free to teach the reformed faith BCO 8-7
par. 11 No record that committee or presbytery heard and approved sermon BCO 19-2
par. 16 Attachment 93A No record of congregational concurrence in dissolving call. BCO 23-1 Because cited for this last year.

par. 19 Attachment 11 Presbytery minutes do not reflect that organizing congregation submitted petition to presbytery or that commission examined officer candidates. Commission minutes fail to list declaration of quorum, charter members taking vows, election of elders. BCO 5-8,9
par. 6 Terms of call not listed. Nor is the record of his acceptance of call. Cited for similar infraction last year.
par. 5 No record of roll. Unacceptable excuse in minutes. Cited for this last year. This is clerk's responsibility.
par. 7 Attachment #1 Improper commission report on a letter reporting is not minutes of commission.
par. 20 Various parts of exam not listed. No record of acceptance of call; no record of commission to install.
par. 12 Various parts of an examination not listed. No declaration that out of bounds TE be allowed to teach Reformed Faith BCO 8-7
No quorum declared. Applicable to all minutes.
Section 19f No record that sermon preached or approved. No record of completed internship. No record of theological and exegetical papers being approved. BCO 21-4
Section 17 no record of theological and exegetical papers. No record of internship. No record of completed education requirements for two of the ordinands. BCO 21-4
top of last page 1, 2nd paragraph. Minutes should be specific that Session consented to Presbytery's appointment of Sessional Moderator.
Enrollment has no mention of excused and unexcused absent churches.
p. 4 h. and i. (See page 10.20.a & b) Text of complaints omitted.
p. 6. o-s. BCO 19-1 states that a minister from another denomination who is a stated supply must be examined for licensure. His examination did not include Bible content and BCO.
Page 6 #17-4 Not in accord with BCO 20 to require a congregation to consult with Presbytery committee before calling a pastor. Preliminary principle #6, BCO 3-1, 11-3, and 13-9.
Page 6 #16c No record of petition to be organized BCO 5-8-1.
Page 7 #30 No record of petition to be organized BCO 5-81.
Page 8 #35 BCO 31:1,2; 16-1,2 suspended elders without cause and took jurisdiction without consent of congregation.
Page 4 #10g.2,a,c,f No record of commissions keeping a record of their proceedings or of them submitting them to the court.
Page 5 No record of Presbytery authorization to dismiss TE's to other Presbyteries. BCO 20-9
Page 13 #20 a.c. No complete record of dissolutions of pastoral relationships.
RAO 14.10 h.5. No up-to-date copy of standing rules of the Presbytery.
No record of report of candidates under care.
No record of commission to install.
response: The Presbytery respectfully disagrees with this exception and notes that the commission was erected at the next meeting (see July 8, 1989, p. 12, 89-72).

description: Presbytery examined candidate for licensure on his views by a commission rather than from the floor (BCO 19-2.C, 15-2).
response: Presbytery apologizes for its error which resulted from our apparent misunderstanding of BCO 15-2; and assures the Assembly that we will not repeat the error. Our understanding is that this exception did not require us to have Mr. Kistler repeat his licensure examinations before Presbytery as a whole. We will not omit any sections of his ordination examinations on the basis of his licensure examinations.

description: Commission to ordain — quorum by Presbytery listed as one TE and one RE. BCO 15-2 requires two TEs and two REs.
response: Presbytery acknowledges its error, and will seek to avoid similar faults in future, and that we understand that a procedural error done in good faith does not invalidate the ordination.

description: No record that TE being received from another denomination was asked the questions of BCO 21-5.
response: The citation should be to section 17.13.3. Appropriate constitutional questions for receiving a minister from another denomination were put to Mr. Shields. He answered all in the affirmative. While the minutes do not cite the BCO address, Grace presbytery would respectfully note that it did comply with the appropriate paragraphs of the BCO in receiving a minister from another denomination.

description: Moderator did not follow BCO 19-4 and 19-10 for applicant for licensure and internship.
response: Grace Presbytery did follow BCO 19-4. The minutes show that the questions of 19-3 were posed and answered and that the Moderator offered prayer as required in 19-4. Grace Presbytery acknowledges that the minutes fail to show that the Moderator addressed the applicants in the words of licensure. This was done. The applicant was enrolled as an intern in accordance with BCO 19-10 at the 63rd meeting effective the 62nd meeting. Grace Presbytery will attempt to avoid such omissions in the minutes in the future.

description: Examination not specified as to content. (BCO 13-6).
response: The Presbytery respectfully agrees with the exception and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: Motion to divest needs a 2/3 vote (BCO 34-10).
response: The Presbytery agrees with the exception and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: No annual report of candidates under care (BCO 21-6).
response: The Presbytery respectfully agrees with the exception and promises to be more careful in the future.

description: Page 125: No record of the membership and Assistance Committee being appointed a Commission. Page 133 says it was the direction of Presbytery, but no record of when, nor was quorum established.
response: Presbytery acknowledges the failure to make sure its minutes adequately explained what took place here. The job description of the Membership and Assistance Committee empowers the committee to act for the Presbytery in between Presbytery meetings in dissolving pastoral relationships and dismissing ministers. The quorum was not defined in the job description; this oversight has now been corrected by action of Presbytery.

description: Page 137: Report of Commission dated 9/25/89 is missing.
response: This is an oversight of the clerk's office. A copy of these minutes is in the Clerk's permanent files and will appear as an addendum to the present minutes.

description: BCO 13-9 dissolution of pastoral relationship should be carried out under guidelines of Rules of Discipline, Part II, Chapters 27-46.
response: North Georgia Presbytery argues the BCO provides for dissolution of the pastoral relationship between a teaching elder and the calling body, even without judicial process, if the interest of religion imperatively demands it. The TE served as organizing pastor called by presbytery, not by the congregation. NGP respectfully requests that the 19th GA rescind the 18th GA's exception. Without admitting error, at its July 21, 1990 meeting NGP rescinded the action to accept the committee's examination, corrected its minutes, and re-received the TEs based on the examination conducted.

description: Presbytery accepted a committee's examination in lieu of an examination on the floor of Presbytery (BCO 13-6).
response: North Georgia Presbytery argues BCO 13-6 does not contain express provisions mandating that transfer examinations be conducted on the floor of Presbytery. Without admitting error, at July 21, 1990 meeting NGP rescinded the action to accept the Membership Committee's examination, corrected the Minutes of April 17, 1990, and received TEs Adams and Waldecker into the Presbytery based upon the examination conducted on April 17, 1990. Requests the 19th GA rescind the 18th GA's exception.

description: Page 6 (3): No listing of TE's exception to WCF. Context: examination of Rev. Michael Alford for reception.
response: Presbytery agrees with the exception and promised to be more careful in the future. TE Alford's exceptions to WCF 21:5 (exclusion of hymns/spiritual songs), WCF 21:8 (strict Sabbatarian interpretation), and WCF 28:7 (rebaptism of those from non-evangelical churches) are listed for the record.

description: Page 7 (7) (8) and page (10): Business acted upon that was not set forth in the call (BCO 23-1).
response: Presbytery agrees with this exception and promises to be more careful in the future. Items (7) and (10) were listed in the Docket published in the Call; only Item (8) was not set forth in the Call. This item was considered as a matter of convenience and the rights of the minister in question were not violated in that he had requested to be dismissed from the PCA.

description: Page 29 (41)1: BCO 32-2 requires full concurrence of Presbytery within whose bounds a TE is laboring out of bounds.
response: Presbytery respectfully disagrees. Item 41(1) is a report given as information only as part of an effort to comply with BCO 13-2. Presbytery was in the process of trying to correct the irregularity but was unsuccessful. Presbytery agrees that BCO 13-2 requires full concurrence but respectfully disagrees that its actions were out of accord with BCO 13-2. Presbytery requests the Assembly's guidance as to how to proceed in this situation.

description: Page 31 (44) 3. p.4: Insufficient grounds for omitting oral sermon (BCO 19-2).
response: Presbytery agrees with this exception and promises to be more careful in the future. The testimonials referred to were from members of Presbytery who had heard Mr. Wong preach.

description: Page 32 (49) 3: No mention of vote of congregation to release pastor. (BCO 23-1). Context: Dissolution of call of Westminster PCA to Rev. John L. Vance.
response: Presbytery agrees with this exception and promises to be more careful in the future. Correspondence Item 13 is a letter from the Clerk of Session stating that matter had been acted on at a congregational meeting May 4, 1989. Presbytery neglected to record this fact in its Minutes.

description: Par. 15b (2): No examination on Sacraments (BCO 19-2).
response: No corrective action taken, or necessary, as the fifth sentence repeated already stated that John Routzahn was examined in the area of the Sacraments at the October 1989 Presbytery meeting.

description: Par. 16.2: Presbytery instructed CE committee to issue a call to TE David Brown to labor in a work apparently outside of Presbytery's jurisdiction.
response: Minutes corrected: the improper call to TE Brown was replaced with a call in proper form at the March 1990 Presbytery meeting. TE Brown was approved to labor outside the bounds of the Presbytery in accordance with the terms of his call and the requirements of BCO 8-7.

description: P. 34,35, 88-82.1 BCO 5-3 states 3 ways to form a 'Temporary Government'. Presbytery used none of the three but a mixture of two.
response: Presbytery respectfully questions the Committee's interpretation. The Organizing Pastor is a member of Presbytery and is eligible to be appointed to a presbytery Commission. The Presbytery believes it is wise to have the Organizing Pastor serve on such a Commission and has used option 3 in BCO 5-3 to provide a temporary government for its Mission churches.

description: P. 36. 89-83. No indication that organizing commission examined prospective ruling elders according to BCO 3-8.
response: Presbytery respectfully disagrees. Minutes show the Commission did interview elders on April 29, 1989, but they are not identified as 'prospective' elders because they were previously ordained by this independent church prior to its petition to be received by Presbytery.

description: 90-14. No approval of theological thesis and exegetical paper. 90-14. No oral examination on Bible content.
response: Candidates & Credentials Committee records show that the Committee did approve the ordinand's thesis and exegetical paper, but simply failed to mention the fact on the floor of Presbytery. The examination on English Bible was actually part of the exam on Theology.

description: The minutes do not record that the session minutes of the member churches were examined during one year period (BCO 12-7, 40-1).
response: The Presbytery of Southeast Alabama acknowledges its error in this matter, and has taken steps to correct its procedure to record in the minutes the names of churches whose Sessional minutes have been examined.

description: Page 90-6, par. 18-b - Exception to WCF noted but not listed.
response: Mr. Tiley's exception noted to the WCF was in the area of Sabbath recreations.

description: No record of TE Graybill's educational qualifications (BCO 13-6, 21-4).
response: Teaching Elder William Graybill has met all the educational requirements of the PCA, and the Presbytery apologizes to the General Assembly for failing to record that fact in its minutes.

description: No record of TE Armerding's educational qualifications (BCO 13-6, 21-4).
response: The Presbytery acknowledges its failure to consider the educational requirements of Dr. Armerding. Therefore, it is moved that the Presbytery make an exception to the seminary education requirements for Hudson Armerding in view of the fact that he has given a lifetime of outstanding service to Christ and His church and was received with status of Honorably Retired (BCO 21-4). The motion which requires a 3/4 vote, carried unanimously.

description: Commission to install TE Kelley not established, or quorum named (BCO 15-2).
response: Presbytery on motion rescinded its decision to appoint a Commission to install TE Kelley. (TE Kelley was given powers of an Evangelist at that meeting, and therefore an installation was not necessary).

description: 17.238: BCO 19-7 through 19-16 requires an internship. Presbytery has not mentioned its completion or equivalency for ordained at the time of his trials for ordination.
response: After checking Minutes, in the paragraph immediately preceding the one stated in the exception, 17.237 stated: 'As permitted in BCO 19-16, the court unanimously approved Mr. Phillips' previous experience as being equivalent to an internship and that no further testing of gifts be required.' Warrior Presbytery unanimously and respectfully disagreed with the exception.

description: Attachments listed in Index, but actual attachments are not available for review (e.g., minutes of commissions, committee reports, resolutions, overtures). This is at least third year in a row same citation noted.
response: Heartland reaffirmed its Policy on Distribution of Presbytery Minutes and responded to 18th GA Review of Presbytery Records with the same answer as last year, respectfully asking that the committee reconsider it. Also noted items re: Directory, Roll, list of men under care and licentiates, and standing rules (RAO 9-14-8). 19th GA: Recommend that 20th GA not seat commissioners from Heartland Presbytery until the Committee on Constitutional Business certifies to the stated clerk that compliance to repeated citation is met.

description: 227 #15 SR were not met (no 2/3 vote). [Exception from 17th GA]
response: On page 227 the S.R. were suspended in re to the membership of the CE/P Committee of Presbytery. The S.R. Were upheld in that there was a 2/3 vote, but, since there was no opposition, the Clerk did not indicate the need for, or the amount. There were 34 RE and 30 TE present. The Clerk shall be careful to indicate the need for and the amount of the 2/3 votes.

description: p. 14 Committee acted for the court without prior commission (BCO 15-1). [Exception from 17th GA]
response: The Care of the Churches Committee/Commission acted for the court with prior commission status (see Motions #7 and #8, pp. 2, 3 Minutes 21-22 October 1988). Motion #7 invited the committee to conclude the matter of Walter Lorenz's uncontested resignation with Commission status. Motion #8 appointed the Care of the Churches Committee as a Committee with Commission status.
